description: AI agent platform with gateway, channels, models, browser automation, and node management.

subcommands.name: onboard
subcommands.description: Interactive onboarding for gateway, workspace, and skills.

arguments.name: Reset
arguments.description: Reset config, credentials, and sessions before onboarding.

arguments.name: Flow
arguments.description: Onboarding flow type (manual is alias for advanced).

arguments.name: Token Provider
arguments.description: Token provider ID (non-interactive; used with --auth-choice token).

arguments.name: Node Manager
arguments.description: Package manager (pnpm recommended; bun not recommended for Gateway runtime).

subcommands.name: configure
subcommands.description: Interactive configuration wizard for models, channels, skills, and gateway.

subcommands.name: doctor
subcommands.description: Health checks and quick fixes for config, gateway, and legacy services.

arguments.name: Non-Interactive
arguments.description: Skip prompts; apply safe migrations only.

arguments.name: Deep
arguments.description: Scan system services for extra gateway installs.

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Show linked session health and recent recipients.

subcommands.name: health
subcommands.description: Fetch health from the running Gateway.

arguments.name: Active
arguments.description: Filter sessions active within N minutes.

arguments.name: Reset
arguments.description: Reset dev config, credentials, sessions, and workspace.

subcommands.name: logs
subcommands.description: Tail Gateway file logs via RPC.

arguments.name: Limit
arguments.description: Number of log lines to show.

subcommands.name: agent
subcommands.description: Run one agent turn via the Gateway (or --local embedded).

arguments.name: To
arguments.description: Destination for session key and optional delivery.

arguments.name: Local
arguments.description: Run agent locally (embedded) instead of via Gateway.

arguments.name: Deliver
arguments.description: Deliver the message to the destination.

subcommands.name: message send
subcommands.description: Send a message to a target.

subcommands.name: channels list
subcommands.description: Show configured channels and auth profiles.

subcommands.name: channels status
subcommands.description: Check gateway reachability and channel health.

subcommands.name: channels add
subcommands.description: Add a channel (wizard-style or non-interactive with flags).

subcommands.name: channels remove
subcommands.description: Disable or remove a channel config entry.

arguments.name: Delete
arguments.description: Remove config entries without prompts (default is disable only).

arguments.name: Channel
arguments.description: Channel to log in to (default whatsapp).

subcommands.name: channels logs
subcommands.description: Show recent channel logs from the gateway log file.

subcommands.name: models status
subcommands.description: Show model auth overview and OAuth expiry status.

arguments.name: Probe
arguments.description: Live probe of configured auth profiles (may consume tokens).

arguments.name: Model
arguments.description: Model ID to set as primary.

subcommands.name: models scan
subcommands.description: Scan for available models across providers.

arguments.name: Set Default
arguments.description: Set the scanned model as default.

subcommands.name: security audit
subcommands.description: Audit config and local state for common security issues.

arguments.name: Fix
arguments.description: Tighten safe defaults and chmod state/config.

subcommands.name: secrets reload
subcommands.description: Re-resolve secret refs and atomically swap the runtime snapshot.

subcommands.name: secrets audit
subcommands.description: Scan for plaintext residues, unresolved refs, and precedence drift.

subcommands.name: secrets apply
subcommands.description: Apply a previously generated secrets plan.

arguments.name: Allow Exec
arguments.description: Permit exec providers in dry-run and exec-containing write plans.

arguments.name: Dry Run
arguments.description: Show what would be reset without doing it.

subcommands.name: uninstall
subcommands.description: Uninstall the gateway service and local data (CLI remains).

arguments.name: Non-Interactive
arguments.description: Non-interactive mode (requires --yes and explicit scopes or --all).

arguments.name: Dry Run
arguments.description: Show what would be uninstalled without doing it.

subcommands.name: skills check
subcommands.description: Summary of ready vs missing skill requirements.

subcommands.name: plugins install
subcommands.description: Install a plugin from path, archive, npm spec, or marketplace.

arguments.name: Source
arguments.description: Plugin path, .tgz, npm spec, or plugin@marketplace.

subcommands.name: node install
subcommands.description: Install the node host as a background service.

subcommands.name: browser screenshot
subcommands.description: Capture a screenshot of the current page or element.

subcommands.name: browser open
subcommands.description: Open a URL in a new tab.

subcommands.name: tui
subcommands.description: Open the terminal UI connected to the Gateway.

arguments.name: History Limit
arguments.description: Number of history entries to load.

arguments.name: Workspace
arguments.description: Agent workspace directory (required in non-interactive mode).

subcommands.name: agents delete
subcommands.description: Delete an agent and prune its workspace and state.

subcommands.name: qr
subcommands.description: Display a QR code for pairing.

subcommands.name: backup create
subcommands.description: Create a backup of config and state.

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Update OpenClaw to the latest version.

arguments.name: Apply
arguments.description: Install/update CoreDNS config (requires sudo; macOS only).

subcommands.name: system event
subcommands.description: Enqueue a system event and optionally trigger a heartbeat.

arguments.name: Dev Mode
arguments.description: Isolate state under ~/.openclaw-dev and shift default ports.

arguments.name: Update
arguments.description: Shorthand for openclaw update (source installs only).
